title: Agent Blueprint IR
description: The composition manifest a selection (one pattern + N primitives + N modifiers, at a scale) compiles to. It is a declarative ASSEMBLY manifest with symbolic *_ref leaves, not a behavioural program: a generator emits procedural code from it. This keeps it framework-agnostic (one IR, many emitters) while sidestepping the 'graph fallacy' of encoding dynamic flow as static data. Each blueprint entry contributes an `ir_fragment` (at its Implementation level); the generator merges the selected fragments into a full IR, namespacing ids by entry and binding each abstract port to a deployments option.

ir_version.description: IR contract version. Additive fields do not bump this; shape changes do.

selection.description: The user selection this IR was composed from.

state.description: The Run-State the graph threads. References a RunState subclass symbolically rather than inlining it.

base.description: Always extends the kernel RunState base (core/interfaces/python/state.py).

schema_ref.description: Symbolic ref to the pattern's RunState subclass, e.g. 'patterns/react/schemas/state.py:ReActState'.

channels.description: State fields written concurrently and how to merge them (LangGraph-style reducers).

ports.description: Abstract ports the composed agent needs; each binds to a deployments option at generation time.

edges.description: Optional. Sequential edges are derived from declaration order + dataflow; declare explicit edges only for router / parallel / loop shapes.

provenance.description: Maps each namespaced step/port id to the blueprint entry that contributed it (for merge-conflict detection).

id.description: Step id, namespaced by entry id (e.g. 'react.loop').

code_ref.description: Symbolic ref to the reference implementation or template the emitter fills (e.g. 'patterns/react/code/python/langgraph/react.py').

config.description: Kind-specific config (model id, prompt-template id, tool name, MCP server, k). Open by design; '${ports.x}' interpolates a bound port.

interrupt.description: Suspend the run for human input before/after this step (the pause-resume seam).

compensate_ref.description: Symbolic ref to this step's saga compensator, unwound on downstream failure.

control_policy.description: Who picks the next step — the workflow (code) vs agent (model) switch.

decision_step.description: For router/planner: the step that chooses the next step.

condition.description: A guard ref evaluated against Run-State.

binding.description: The deployments option bound to this port, filled at composition time (e.g. 'memory_store.pgvector'). Empty in a blueprint fragment.

cross_cutting.description: A cross-cutting concern wrapping the graph. Blueprints own the design-time shape; deployments own the operational realization (the binding).

ref.description: Symbolic ref to the modifier/handler that realizes it.

when.description: Optional guard ref; absent = unconditional. The emitter turns this into an `if`, keeping data-dependent branching out of static edges.
